creator: freebsd@grem.de
text:
On Wed, 08 Jan 2014 22:34:06 +0100
Matthias Andree <mandree@FreeBSD.org> wrote:

> Michael,
> 
> It would appear that "make WITH_BDB_VER=6" triggers this, and
> WITH_BDB_VER might be in /etc/make.conf (and better be written as
> "WITH_BDB_HIGHEST=yes").
> 
> I am proposing the attached patch, which crucially changes
> 
> 	INVALID_BDB_VER=        2 3 40 41 42 43 44 46 47 48
> 
> to
> 
> 	INVALID_BDB_VER=        4 40 41 42 43 44 46 47 48 6
> 
> in order to prevent the ports system from grabbing db6.
> 2 and 3 are already gone and need no longer be excluded.
> 
> Do you approve of this change?
> 
> 
> The attached patch also makes some cleanups for the new ports world
> order that I have blanket approval for (LIB_DEPENDS arguments now list
> the library's filename, and USE_GMAKE=yes now is listed in USES=).
> 
> Best regards
> Matthias

Hi Matthias,

I do approve of the patch (INVALID_BDB_VER) and trust that
LIB_DEPENDS is what we want, but it would be *really* good if this could
be merged with the patch I made to upgrade to Ice 3.5.1, that
also introduces staging and other new world features available
here http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/184453 and most
importantly allows to build Ice on the upcoming 10-RELEASE.

Cheers,
Michael



-- 
Michael Gmelin
